result.reasoning: A thorough search for propositions and chunks related to "CENP-B inhibits heterochromatin formation" and "CENP-B heterochromatin" did not yield any direct evidence supporting or refuting the claim. The search results primarily discussed general mechanisms of heterochromatin formation involving other proteins (e.g., HP1, HRP2, LBR, MBDs) or focused on a different protein (Cbl-b). There was no information found regarding CENP-B's role in inhibiting heterochromatin formation. Therefore, based on the available evidence, there is insufficient information to verify the claim.

evidence.text: Phosphorylated PLCγ1 may serve as a potential biomarker associated with the inhibition of Cbl-b function.

evidence.text: In constitutive heterochromatin regions, H3K9 methylation reinforces heterochromatin silencing by recruiting DNA methyltransferases and promoting DNA methylation (Dong et al. 2008; Leung et al. 2011; Xin et al. 2003).

evidence.text: In facultative heterochromatin regions, H3K27 methylation typically antagonizes DNA methylation (Fu et al. 2020; van Mierlo et al. 2019).
